mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during consecutive rate hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 4.0%, it signals a labor market inflection that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy decays to chance within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of housing starts and real credit flows indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 3-year regression, or forward returns do not diverge significantly between high/low HOUST-RSAFS co-decline states.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress periods, a simultaneous rise in industrial production and employment validates capacity expansion, which positively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days by confirming earnings resilience.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS rise concurrently, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the consumer price index erode real household purchasing power and compress consumer discretionary margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains positive over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during CPI acceleration phases are not significantly lower than periods of CPI deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged corporate expansions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 3.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while housing starts decline signals a structural shift toward services consumption and away from capital-intensive construction, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between PAYEMS growth and HOUST levels shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or the signal generates negative forward returns in more than 55% of divergence instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary regime where real GDP growth stagnates while industrial production remains elevated, inventory drawdowns force firms to raise prices, temporarily boosting equity valuations over the next 5 trading days before margin compression sets in.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0% while INDPRO remains above 105, or the hypothesis fails to produce a positive information coefficient over any 1-year rolling window.
